examples.x: The driver of the car opened the car door for the passengers.
The Uber driver asked me for the destination before starting the trip.

senses.definition: someone who uses a motorcycle or bicycle for transportation

examples.x: The rider zipped through traffic on his motorcycle.
She is an experienced rider who competes in cycling races.
Bicycle riders must follow traffic rules for safety.
Many riders prefer using motorcycles for their daily commute.
The mountain trail attracted riders from all over the region.

senses.definition: someone who rides a motorcycle or bicycle

examples.x: The biker sped down the highway on his motorcycle.
Every weekend, the biker joins his friends for a long ride.
The biker wore a helmet and leather jacket for safety.
The biker navigated through the traffic with ease.
He admired the new model of the bike that the seasoned biker was riding.
The cyclist pedaled quickly up the steep hill.
Every morning, the cyclist rides through the park for exercise.
The cyclist navigated through the city streets with confidence.
He fixed a flat tire for a fellow cyclist during their ride.
The cyclist stopped at the intersection to wait for the traffic light.

senses.definition: someone who drives a car or other motor vehicle

examples.x: The motorist carefully followed the traffic rules.
She became a motorist after passing her driving test last month.
He helped a fellow motorist jump-start a dead battery.
Every day, the motorist commutes to work using the highway.
The motorist wore a seatbelt and checked the mirrors before starting the car.
The motorcyclist zoomed past the cars on the highway.
A group of motorcyclists gathered at the diner before their ride.
The motorcyclist skillfully maneuvered through the busy traffic.
She became a motorcyclist after buying her first bike last year.
Every weekend, the motorcyclist explores new scenic routes.

senses.definition: a skilled driver who competes in racing events, maneuvering vehicles at high speeds around tracks or courses

examples.x: During the race, the racer executed a daring overtake on the final lap.
Every racer must balance aggression with control to succeed on the track.
The racer adjusted their racing line to optimize speed through the corners.
The racer's experience and reflexes were evident in their quick reactions on track.
The young racer dreams of reaching the highest levels of motorsport competition.

senses.definition: someone who drives a truck or operates other heavy vehicles, often for transporting goods

examples.x: The teamster drove the truck across the country to deliver the goods.
Every day, the teamster starts his shift before dawn.
The teamster union negotiates better working conditions for its members.
He assisted a fellow teamster with a breakdown on the highway.
The teamster stopped at the weigh station to ensure the truck was within legal limits.

senses.definition: someone who drives a truck, usually for long distances, to transport goods

examples.x: The trucker drove all night to make the delivery on time.
Every week, the trucker travels across several states.
A group of truckers gathered at the rest area to share stories.
The trucker stopped at a diner for a quick meal during his route.
During the winter, truckers often face challenging driving conditions.

senses.definition: an individual who operates large vehicles to transport goods over long distances

examples.x: The truck driver delivered the furniture to my new house this morning.
He works as a truck driver, traveling long distances every week.
My uncle is a truck driver, and he travels across the country for his job.
She became a truck driver after finishing her driving courses.
The truck driver took a break at the rest stop to stretch and grab some food.

senses.definition: a person who drives a taxi to transport passengers for a set price

examples.x: The cabby picked up passengers from the busy airport.
Every night, the cabby works late shifts in the city.
The cabby skillfully navigated through heavy traffic.
During rush hour, cabbies often have to deal with impatient passengers and traffic jams.
The cabby stopped at a gas station to refuel before picking up the next passenger.

senses.definition: someone who is employed to drive someone else's car for them

examples.x: The chauffeur picked up the CEO from the airport in a luxury sedan.
She hired a chauffeur to drive her to the wedding venue in style.
As a professional chauffeur, he takes pride in providing a safe and comfortable ride for his clients.
The chauffeur's job includes maintaining the cleanliness and upkeep of the vehicle.
The hotel offers chauffeur services to guests who require transportation around the city.

senses.definition: someone who drives aggressively or selfishly, often taking up more space on the road than necessary

examples.x: The road hog cut off several cars while changing lanes without signaling.
Every morning, the road hog speeds through traffic, causing delays.
The road hog ignored traffic signals and drove recklessly.
Other drivers honked at the road hog for blocking the intersection.
He encountered a road hog on his way to work, forcing him to slow down.

senses.definition: someone who rides alongside or ahead of a convoy or group, typically to provide protection or guidance

examples.x: The outrider led the procession of vehicles through the crowded streets.
The outrider wore a high-visibility vest and communicated with hand signals.
She volunteered to be an outrider during the charity bike ride to ensure safety.
The outrider cleared the way for the VIP convoy by managing traffic.
He admired the skill of the outrider as they maneuvered through narrow lanes.

senses.definition: someone whose job is parking customers' cars at restaurants or hotels

examples.x: As the hotel guests pulled up to the entrance, the valet swiftly took their keys and parked their car in the designated area.
The valet greeted guests with a warm smile as he handed back their keys upon their return from dinner.
She tipped the valet generously for his prompt service in retrieving her car during rush hour.
The valet carefully maneuvered the expensive sports car into a parking spot, ensuring it was safe and secure.

senses.definition: a person chosen to abstain from alcohol and ensure the safe transportation of others who have been drinking

examples.x: John agreed to be the designated driver, showing his commitment to responsible drinking habits.
Mark volunteered to be the designated driver for the night out with friends.
Our group always appoints a designated driver to make sure we get home safely after a party.
Lisa, the non-drinker in the group, was the designated driver.
The night's designated driver is key for a safe celebration.

senses.definition: someone traveling in a vehicle, aircraft, ship, etc. who is not the pilot, driver, or a crew member

examples.x: She often reads a book to pass the time when she's a passenger on long road trips.
The bus passenger pressed the button to request the next stop.
The bus was crowded with passengers during the morning commute.
The flight attendant demonstrated safety procedures to the passengers.
The passenger on the cruise ship enjoyed a view of the ocean from her cabin.

senses.definition: a member of a motorcycle club known for its distinctive style and culture

examples.x: He looks like a Hells Angel with his leather jacket and motorcycle boots.
The Hells Angel revved his engine loudly as he pulled into the parking lot.
People often mistake him for a Hells Angel because of his rugged appearance and love for motorcycles.
The Hells Angel greeted his fellow club members with a firm handshake and a nod.
